title: Longest subarray with a given sum

content.problemStatement:
You are given an array of integers numsnumsnums with length nnn and an integer kkk. Your task is to determine the length of the longest continuous sub-array whose elements sum up exactly to kkk. If there is no such sub-array, return 000.

**Function Declaration****Function Name**longestSubarraySumlongestSubarraySumlongestSubarraySum – This function computes the length of the longest continuous sub-array whose sum is exactly equal to kkk.

**Parameters**
arrarrarr : A vector of integers of length nnn, representing the array.
kkk : An integer representing the required target sum.
**Return Value**
Returns a single integer —
the **maximum length** of any continuous sub-array whose sum equals kkk.
If no such sub-array exists, the function returns 000.
`The input and output formats given below are only if you want to test using custom inputs.`

**Constraints**
1≤T≤1051 \leq T \leq 10^51≤T≤105
1≤n≤1051 \leq n \leq 10^51≤n≤105
−105≤arr[i]≤105-10^5 \leq arr[i] \leq 10^5−105≤arr[i]≤105
−109≤k≤109-10^9 \leq k \leq 10^9−109≤k≤109

Input Format


The first line of input will contain a single integer **TTT**, denoting the number of test cases.




Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.



The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers **nnn** and **kkk** — the length of the array and the required sum respectively.
The next line contains **nnn** space-separated integers, representing the array **`nums`**.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line a single integer —
the **length of the longest continuous subarray whose sum is exactly kkk**.
If no such subarray exists, output **0**.
constraints: Sum of n over all test cases does not exceed 10^5
content.inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case:
- The first line contains two integers n and k — the length of the array and the required sum.
- The second line contains n space‑separated integers, the elements of the array.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single integer on its own line — the maximum length of a continuous sub‑array whose sum equals k. If no such sub‑array exists, output 0.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: sub‑array [5,2,7,1] sums to 15, length 4.\nTest case 2: no sub‑array sums to 6, answer 0.\nTest case 3: sub‑array [2,3] (or [3,2]) sums to 5, length 2.
The sub‑array [1, -1, 2, -2] (indices 0‑3) sums to 0 and is the longest such sub‑array, length 4.
Single element 5 does not equal k=10, so no valid sub‑array exists.

solution.approach: For each test case we scan the array while maintaining the running prefix sum. A hash map stores the earliest index at which each prefix sum value appears. If the current prefix sum equals k, the sub‑array from the start to the current position is a candidate. Otherwise, if (prefix‑sum - k) has been seen before at index j, the sub‑array (j+1 … i) sums to k and its length is i‑j. We keep the maximum length found. This runs in O(n) time and O(n) extra space per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(n) per test case, where n is the length of the array. Overall O(total N) across all test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(n) for the hash map storing prefix sums (at most one entry per index).
